paragraphs: You have a Greek toe form with wide forefoot and a medium-width heel. A mixed profile; forefoot and heel require different fits.

Beyond the obvious: Given your Greek toes, rather avoid very pointy shoes. These might squeeze your toe tips, even if the width of the shoe fits you. Your shallow heel doesn't project as far back as most. Deeply sculpted cups will feel empty at the back. Your big toe drifts slightly inwards (could be a mild hallux valgus), hence we recommend a slightly wider and not too asymmetric shape.
interpretation.title: Your Foot Shape

paragraphs: Your Scarpa Instinct VS (2 down) is rather aggressive for Scarpa; your Scarpa Instinct VSR (2 down) is rather aggressive for Scarpa; your La Sportiva Skwama Vegan (1.5 down) is rather relaxed for La Sportiva; your Wild Climb Mangusta (2 down) is rather aggressive for Wild Climb; your Wild Climb Pantera (2023) (2 down) is rather aggressive for Wild Climb.

Your La Sportiva Skwama Vegan's loose heel, loose toes, and loose forefoot are most likely a sizing problem, you could try a size smaller for a better fit. Your Wild Climb Mangusta's loose could be caused by your shallow heel, the back of your foot may not project far enough to fill the cup which you perceive as loose feel.

Your Wild Climb Pantera (2023)'s heel feels empty while the other shoes fit on the heel. Your Wild Climb Pantera (2023)'s medium volume matches your normal heel width, but your shallow heel may not fill deeply sculpted cups. The cause is likely heel depth, so try shoes with a flatter, less sculpted heel cup.
interpretation.title: What Your Current Shoe Fit Tells Us

paragraphs: Based on your scan and current shoe fit, we target shoes with Greek toe form, normal forefoot width (adjusted from wide scan result based on your current shoe fit), and normal heel width.

Given your preference for bouldering outdoors on mixed and a moderate fit, we prioritize soft slippers or velcros with moderate downturn and slight asymmetry (shifted lower to accommodate your mild hallux valgus).
Note: data on shoe heel depth is unfortunately not widely available. We're working on extracting this data from shoe scans, but for now the recommendations match heel width only.
The softer, stiffer, and budget tiers below offer alternative options around this target.
interpretation.title: What to Look For

recommendations.description: Velcro with Roman toe form, medium fit throughout. 4mm Vibram XS Grip rubber with a partial, soft midsole, resulting in a medium-soft shoe with moderate downturn and slight asymmetry.
recommendations.tradeoffs: Tradeoff: Roman toe form might not be a perfect fit for your Greek toe.
